name: Yerevan (Armenia)

colors.symbolism: drawn from the city's coat of arms — the colour of the lion's field
the gold of the lion and crown on the city arms
the city's coat of arms and Mount Ararat in the background
the snowy peak of Mount Ararat depicted in the arms

description: A blue field with twelve small gold stars in a circular arrangement around the central coat of arms of Yerevan — a crowned golden lion before Mount Ararat — bordered above and below by the cross-stitched ornamental band of an Armenian carpet.
history: Adopted on 27 September 2004 by the Yerevan City Council, simultaneously with a new coat of arms for the city. Yerevan is both the capital and one of the eleven first-level subdivisions (administered as a separate city of republican significance) of Armenia.

features.symbolism: a crowned golden lion in front of Mount Ararat — the symbol of the historical kingdom of Armenia restored to the modern city
the twelve historical capitals of Armenia
